workspaceNote: This workspace (LTS Stand Alone Software) is a full copy of Development/Business Website/Website Code/v23, including .git and origin remote. v23 itself was not modified. Standing instruction: everything stays local to this workspace for the entire build -- no push, no merge into a new vN folder or main -- until Dylan says otherwise. Standing instruction (2026-07-14): work continues session-by-session, unattended, for as long as productive, without stopping to ask -- owner-decision blockers are documented, not asked about. See DECISION_LOG.md.

filesRequiringReview: docs/development/ARCHITECTURE.md (target architecture proposal, frontend build-step recommendation)
knownRisks: No organization/tenant concept exists in the pre-Care-Hub codebase at all -- F001 is a from-scratch build, not an extension
Netlify Blobs has no secondary indexes; list()-scan pattern may not scale to Care Hub query needs
No payment webhook infrastructure exists at all -- F028's state machine is ready but has nothing to drive it
Customer documents/images are stored as base64 data URIs in Blobs, not object storage with signed URLs
Two independent, near-duplicated single-file staff/customer console apps (admin.html, myaccount.html) -- still not extracted
F-numbering collision between audit findings (F001-F036) and Business Care Hub functions (F001-F060) -- mitigated via naming convention
src/audit/blobsAuditSink.js and src/settings/blobsSettingsStore.js are written but not integration-tested against live Netlify Blobs
src/policy/rbac.js's ROLE_CAPABILITIES map is a transcription of the Roles & Permissions sheet's prose, not a workbook (none exists yet)
src/policy/fileValidation.js's size limits and MIME allowlist are engineering defaults, not owner-approved policy

src/policy/triageEngine.js, priorityScoring.js, pricingEngine.js, entitlementCheck.js, readinessChecklist.js are all rule/weight/content INTERPRETERS with no real business configuration behind them yet -- by Session 5, this is the majority of the codebase's logic layer
src/policy/ticketLifecycle.js's 14-day reopen window and src/reminders/lifecycleReminders.js's 30-day reminder window are engineering defaults, not owner-approved policy

None of Sessions 2-5's pure logic modules are wired to real data yet -- all tested against synthetic fixtures only; first real integration test happens once F001 persistence exists and an actual endpoint calls them

F046/F047's readiness/MFA checklists must stay strictly assessment-only (record whether a protection exists, never the protection's actual value/credential) -- this is enforced structurally today (ChecklistResponse.met is boolean-only) but needs to stay that way through every future session that touches this code, per the product's explicit non-goals (not a password manager, not a remote-monitoring agent)
